title: Thetorical Framework: Decision Making Under Uncertainty

body.text: This paper examines how neurodivergent individuals process uncertainty and make decisions, proposing a thetorical framework that accounts for cognitive differences in risk assessment and decision-making under uncertainty.

Standard decision-making models often fail to account for neurodivergent cognitive profiles. This work develops a framework that better describes how individuals with autism, ADHD, and related conditions evaluate risk and make choices.
Enhanced pattern recognition in certain domains
This framework informs the design of P31 Labs artifacts to better support decision-making processes for neurodivergent users, reducing cognitive load and improving usability.
